title: Arithmetic Progressions
summary: Chapter 5 introduces Arithmetic Progressions (AP) — sequences where each term differs from the previous by a fixed common difference. Students learn to identify APs, find the nth term using the formula aₙ = a + (n-1)d, and calculate the sum of n terms using Sₙ = n/2[2a + (n-1)d]. The chapter includes word problems involving APs in real-life situations such as savings, distances, and patterns.
learningPoints: Define an arithmetic progression as a sequence with constant common difference
Identify whether a given sequence is an AP by checking constant difference
Find the first term a and common difference d of an AP
Apply the nth term formula: aₙ = a + (n-1)d
Find number of terms in a finite AP
Apply alternative sum formula: Sₙ = n/2(a + l) where l is the last term
Find the nth term when sum of n terms is given: aₙ = Sₙ - Sₙ₋₁
Solve problems involving AP in real-life contexts
Find three numbers in AP when their sum and product are given

formulas.label: nth Term of AP
formulas.description: Find any term of AP where a = first term, d = common difference, n = term number.

formulas.label: Sum of n Terms (Method 1)
formulas.description: Sum of first n terms of AP using first term and common difference.

formulas.label: Sum of n Terms (Method 2)
formulas.description: Sum of first n terms using first term a and last term l.

formulas.label: Common Difference
formulas.description: Difference between any two consecutive terms is constant.

formulas.label: nth Term from Sum
formulas.description: Find nth term when sum formula is given.

formulas.label: Three Terms in AP
formulas.description: Three numbers in AP assumed as a-d, a, a+d to simplify calculations.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an AP?
mcqs.explanation: 3, 7, 11, 15 has common difference 4. Others are geometric or triangular sequences.

If 7th term of AP is 34 and 13th term is 64, the common difference is:

exercises.question: Find the 31st term of AP whose 11th term is 38 and 16th term is 73.
An AP has 37 terms. Sum of 3 middle terms is 225 and sum of last 3 terms is 429. Find AP.
exercises.answer: Middle terms are 18th, 19th, 20th. Sum = 3a₁₉ = 225, a₁₉ = 75. Last 3: a₃₅+a₃₆+a₃₇ = 429, 3a₃₆ = 429, a₃₆ = 143. d = (143-75)/17 = 4. a = 75-18(4) = 3. AP: 3, 7, 11, ...

exercises.question: Find sum of all 3-digit numbers divisible by 7.
200 logs are stacked in rows. Bottom row has 20 logs, each row has 1 less. How many rows and logs in top row?
exercises.answer: a = 20, d = -1. Sₙ = n/2[40-(n-1)] = 200. n² - 41n + 400 = 0. (n-16)(n-25) = 0. n = 16 (since top row can't be negative). Top row = 20-15 = 5 logs.

exercises.question: In an AP, if pth term is q and qth term is p, find (p+q)th term.
exercises.answer: aₚ = a+(p-1)d = q and aᵩ = a+(q-1)d = p. Subtracting: (p-q)d = q-p, d = -1. a = q-(p-1)(-1) = q+p-1. aₚ₊ᵩ = (p+q-1) + (p+q-1)(-1) = 0.

exercises.question: A man repays a loan of ₹3250 by paying ₹20 in first month and increases payment by ₹15 each month. How many months to repay?
The ratio of sums of m and n terms of an AP is m²:n². Show ratio of mth and nth terms is (2m-1):(2n-1).
exercises.answer: Sₘ/Sₙ = m²/n². Using Sₙ = n/2[2a+(n-1)d]: [2a+(m-1)d]/[2a+(n-1)d] = m/n. For mth term ratio: replace m by 2m-1 and n by 2n-1: aₘ/aₙ = (2m-1)/(2n-1). Proved.
